project.name: vibe-pm
project.description: An OpenCode plugin that replaces full AGENTS.md/rules loading with step-aware precision context injection — trimming messages to the current flow step, injecting minimal but relevant context, and collecting per-step metrics.

nodes.label: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore rules excluding node_modules, dist, .vibe-pm runtime data, IDE/OS artifacts, logs, env files, coverage, and generated docs/flow and docs/regulation directories.
nodes.name: .gitignore

nodes.label: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project documentation covering tech stack (TypeScript, AxioDB, Zod, tiktoken), directory structure, code map (VibePMPlugin entry, FlowEngine, MemorySystem, TemplateManager), slash commands (/pm-*), core concepts (dual-layer memory, FSM-driven tasks, context injection, per-step metrics), design principles (precision over volume, LLM-driven flow), and anti-patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Written in mixed Chinese/English; code identifiers in English, docs and communication in Chinese per Constitution article VIII.
nodes.name: AGENTS.md

nodes.label: docs/
nodes.summary: Documentation root containing specs, flow definitions, regulations, templates, plans, and notes.
nodes.name: docs

nodes.label: docs/flow/
nodes.summary: Installed flow definitions generated by /pm-install-flow. Excluded from version control.
nodes.name: docs/flow

nodes.label: docs/regulation/
nodes.summary: Project regulations including constitution, coding style, checklists, and dictionary. Excluded from version control.
nodes.name: docs/regulation

nodes.label: docs/spec/
nodes.summary: Program specification documents (8 modules) — the authoritative design source; check before modifying code.
nodes.name: docs/spec

nodes.label: docs/template/
nodes.summary: Built-in flow template library (5 templates) used by /pm-install-flow to populate docs/flow/.
nodes.name: docs/template

nodes.label: rules/
nodes.summary: OpenCode session rules loaded per conversation — project-specific behavioral rules (research, spec-driven, spec-template).
nodes.name: rules

nodes.label: docs/flow/test-flow.md
nodes.summary: Example flow for Flow Engine unit testing. Simulates a typical research→design→review→implement workflow with 4 steps (S1 Understand, S2 Design, S3 Human-in-loop Review, S4 Implement) and a Mermaid stateDiagram defining transitions including back-edges from HiL review.
nodes.languageNotes: Command: /pm-test. Contains Mermaid stateDiagram with HiL step at S3.
nodes.name: docs/flow/test-flow.md

nodes.label: docs/regulation/coding_style.md
nodes.summary: TypeScript coding style regulation: UTF-8/LF, 2-space indent, Prettier, kebab-case filenames, camelCase variables, PascalCase types/classes/interfaces, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE module constants. Strict type safety (no any, no @ts-ignore, no empty catch), function design (object params over 3 args), async/await over raw promises, structured logging with project logger, TODO/FIXME/HACK placeholder conventions. Import grouping: Node built-ins → third-party → project internal.
nodes.name: docs/regulation/coding_style.md

nodes.label: docs/regulation/constitution.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project constitution (v1.0.0) with 8 non-negotiable principles: (I) Flow+Spec+Test-Driven Development, (II) Type Safety Supreme — NO any/@ts-ignore/empty catch, (III) Mandatory Verification via LSP/build/tests, (IV) Mermaid Diagrams Only for architecture/flow/state/sequence charts, (V) Module Independence via explicit interfaces, (VI) Minimal Change — bug fixes are not refactors, (VII) SDK-First Development via @opencode-ai/plugin, (VIII) Chinese-First Communication. Includes zero-tolerance violations table, regulation hierarchy with conflict resolution (constitution > specific rules > oral convention), and directory constraints.
nodes.name: docs/regulation/constitution.md

nodes.label: docs/regulation/dictionary.md
nodes.summary: Terminology dictionary providing Chinese-English mappings for core concepts (Flow/Step/Task/Regulation), task model (Spec/Plan/State), memory system (MD Document Memory/Structured Memory/Discussion/FlowMetrics), flow concepts (FSM/Human-in-loop/Step Transition/Context Injection), tech stack (Plugin/Host/Session/TUI), and command reference table.
nodes.name: docs/regulation/dictionary.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-flow-engine.md
nodes.summary: Spec for Flow Engine — the core business layer. Defines three-layer sandwich context injection (Layer 1 global overview ~900T with Constitution + FSM diagram + step summaries, Layer 2 current step details ~900T with instructions + regulations + task state, Layer 3 lookahead window 0-600T for consecutive non-HiL steps), injection fingerprint deduplication via MD5(flow:step:regulations), three-step message pruning pipeline (step-tagging → depth-level assignment → token-constrained execution with protection mechanisms), lazy pruning at 80% threshold, LLM-driven FSM transitions (no hardcoded rules), FlowDefinition/StepDefinition/InjectedContext domain models, caching strategy targeting 80%+ hit rate (saving 40-60% tokens), and test specifications for task-start, context-injection, flow-parser, and message-pruner.
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-flow-engine.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-memory-system.md
nodes.summary: Spec for Memory System — the data persistence layer using AxioDB v9.6.6 embedded database (JSON file storage). Defines three core data models: Task (sessionId PK, flow, currentStep, closed, summary, specRef, planRef), Discussion (id, fromSessionId, priority 1-5, issue/reason/solution, decision), FlowMetrics (sessionId, flow, step, stepInCount, tokensConsumed, dwellTime, humanInterventionTime, userInputTokens). Key findings: AxioDB UpdateOne() uses plain objects (no $set), queries return data.documents wrapper, single-process instance limit. Includes task lifecycle state machine (Created→Running→Completed), data file organization (.vibe-pm/data.json), CRUD interfaces, and test specs for all three models plus data-file resilience.
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-memory-system.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-metrics-analysis.md
nodes.summary: Spec for Metrics & Analysis — per-step data collection and post-task analysis. Defines IMetricsCollector (onStepEnter/onStepExit), IFlowAnalyzer (analyze/getFlowSummary), StepTokenBreakdown decomposing tokens into 7 buckets (injected context, user messages, tool calls, tool results, LLM responses, pruned tokens, cache hits/misses), StickyTokenCache with 5-min TTL for debouncing, bottleneck identification rules (dwellTime>30%, stepInCount>3, humanIntervention>50%), and Discussion auto-generation from detected patterns. Analysis is deterministic rule-based, not LLM-driven. Token estimation falls back to char/4 when OpenCode API unavailable (75%+ accuracy). Currently Draft status.
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-metrics-analysis.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-overall.md
nodes.summary: Overall system design spec. Identifies two core pain points: (1) mandatory full loading of AGENTS.md and rules/*.md wasting context windows, (2) opaque auto-pruning tools that black-box important content. Vision: task-driven precision context injection, continuous flow optimization via metrics, fragment-time friendly async discussion review, multi-language support. System architecture diagram with 6 layers: Plugin Core (entry/lifecycle), Flow Engine (FSM/context), Memory System (AxioDB JSON), Metrics & Analysis, TUI Display, Template Manager. Defines .vibe-pm.json configuration schema, complete directory structure, module data flow directions (write/read paths), and security policy (read-only file ops, no deletes, no network).
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-overall.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-plugin-core.md
nodes.summary: Spec for Plugin Core — the entry layer handling init, command registration (/pm-* via dual config+tool hooks), configuration loading (.vibe-pm.json with deep merge and defaults), lifecycle hook orchestration (config→event→chat.message→system.transform→messages.transform→session.idle), logging with [vibe-pm] prefix on console.warn/error. Technical stack: TypeScript ES2022 with NodeNext ESM via pnpm, vitest testing, zod validation. Module integration via simple TypeScript import/export (no DI container). Implemented with 4 command stubs remaining. Test specs cover config loading (defaults, overrides, corruption), plugin init (data dir creation, hook registration, module failure isolation), and command registration (completeness, idempotency).
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-plugin-core.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-roadmap.md
nodes.summary: Delivery roadmap with 4 phases: Phase 1 (4 weeks) MVP core — Plugin Core + Memory System + Flow Engine with sandwich injection and message pruning; Phase 2 (1 week) Template Manager with 5 built-in templates; Phase 3 (2 weeks) Metrics & Analysis + TUI Display; Phase 4 (1 week) documentation polish and release. Milestones: M1 MVP ready, M2 out-of-box usable, M3 full features, M4 v1.0 release. Module dependency graph shows FE depends on PC+MS, MA depends on MS+FE, TUI depends on MS. Risk mitigation table for API changes, AxioDB instability, LLM misjudgment, prompt cache hit rate, and pruning false positives.
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-roadmap.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-template-manager.md
nodes.summary: Spec for Template Manager — template scanning, installation, and uninstallation using path convention (/docs/template/ → /docs/flow/) as the sole interface. Defines template file format with Template ID, Category, Command metadata fields. /pm-install-flow workflow: scan templates → present list → copy to /docs/flow/ → auto-generate .opencode/commands/{command}.md prompt file (with scenario, input requirements, execution steps). /pm-uninstall-flow cleans both flow file and command file. 5 built-in templates: research (7 steps), project-build (9 steps), new-feature (13 steps with 4 HiL gates), bug-fix (10 steps), large-refactor (14 steps with 3 HiL gates + migration-checklist regulation). No ITemplateManager abstraction — pure file operations by convention.
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-template-manager.md

nodes.label: docs/spec/vibe-pm-tui-display.md
nodes.summary: Spec for TUI Display — terminal status panel showing active task info (flow, current step, elapsed time, step timing bar chart with HiL markers, token consumption). Data model: TUIState → TaskDisplay → StepTiming. Read-only consumer of Memory System; no writes. Rendering approach TBD (Option A: system prompt injection into OpenCode sidebar; Option B: independent ANSI process). Refresh at conversation boundaries, not real-time. Currently Draft status. Test specs for active task rendering, empty state, elapsed calculation, current step highlighting, and token aggregation.
nodes.name: docs/spec/vibe-pm-tui-display.md

nodes.label: docs/template/flow-template.md
nodes.summary: Flow document template — copy to /docs/flow/ to create a new flow. Defines the canonical format: header (Command, Version, Date), 适用场景 (applicable scenarios), 输入要求 (input requirements table), 默认交付清单 (default deliverables), 状态机 (Mermaid stateDiagram with HiL notes), and 任务步骤 (numbered steps with 目标/执行 Agent/引用 Regulation/instructions/完成后). Conventions: ⚠️ in step title + blockquote for HiL, onComplete describes transition conditions.
nodes.name: docs/template/flow-template.md

nodes.label: docs/template/bug-fix/
nodes.summary: Bug fix template bundle — 10-step flow from root cause analysis through fix, regression test, self-check, human acceptance, and merge.
nodes.name: docs/template/bug-fix

nodes.label: docs/template/bug-fix/flow.md
nodes.summary: Bug fix flow template (Template ID: bug-fix, Command: /pm-bug-fix, Category: maintenance). 10-step FSM: S1 Understand Bug → S2 Root Cause Analysis → S3 [HiL] Review Plan → S4 Write Fix → S5 Write Tests → S6 Regression → S7 Self-Check → S8 [HiL] User Acceptance → S9 Merge. Back-edges from S7/S8 to S4 and S3←S2. Emphasizes minimal fix (no unrelated refactoring per Constitution VI), regression test coverage, and tsc --noEmit validation.
nodes.name: docs/template/bug-fix/flow.md

nodes.label: docs/template/large-refactor/
nodes.summary: Large-scale refactor template bundle — 14-step flow with impact assessment, migration path design, backward compatibility, and compatibility verification. Includes migration-checklist regulation.
nodes.name: docs/template/large-refactor

nodes.label: docs/template/large-refactor/flow.md
nodes.summary: Large refactor flow template (Template ID: large-refactor, Command: /pm-large-refactor, Category: development). 14-step FSM: S1 Understand Intent → S2 Explore Existing Code → S3 Mark Impact & Risk → S4 [HiL] Progressive Interview → S5 Design Plan & Migration Path → S6 [HiL] Review Plan → S7 Write Code (backward compatible) → S8 Write Migration Tests → S9 Run Tests/Fix → S10 Compatibility Verification → S11 Self-Check → S12 [HiL] User Acceptance → S13 Merge. Uses explore/librarian agents for code discovery, mandates Adapter/Facade pattern for backward compatibility, includes Deprecation lifecycle. Largest template (14 steps, 3 HiL gates).
nodes.name: docs/template/large-refactor/flow.md

nodes.label: docs/template/large-refactor/regulations/
nodes.summary: Regulations bundled with the large-refactor template.
nodes.name: docs/template/large-refactor/regulations

nodes.label: docs/template/large-refactor/regulations/migration-checklist.md
nodes.summary: Migration compatibility verification checklist bundled with large-refactor template. Covers: interface compatibility (old APIs via Adapter/Facade, deprecation warnings), data compatibility (read old format, bidirectional conversion, rollback support), test coverage (regression, new behavior, compatibility layer, edge cases), and migration path (documented steps, stakeholder notification, deprecation milestones). Used in S10 Compatibility Verification step.
nodes.name: docs/template/large-refactor/regulations/migration-checklist.md

nodes.label: docs/template/new-feature/
nodes.summary: New feature template bundle — 13-step comprehensive development flow with 4 HiL gates for progressive clarification and review.
nodes.name: docs/template/new-feature

nodes.label: docs/template/new-feature/flow.md
nodes.summary: New feature development flow template (Template ID: new-feature, Command: /pm-new-feature, Category: development). 13-step FSM based on XMind heavy-task workflow: S1 Understand Requirements → S2 Mark Info Gaps → S3 [HiL] Progressive Interview → S4 Design Spec Changes → S5 [HiL] Confirm Changes → S6 Design Plan → S7 [HiL] Review Plan → S8 Write Code → S9 Write Tests & Fix → S10 Self-Check → S11 [HiL] Review & Confirm → S12 Deliver & Merge. Requires existing Spec document as input. 4 human-in-loop gates, back-edges from S10/S11 to S8, from S9 to S9, and from S5 to S3.
nodes.name: docs/template/new-feature/flow.md

nodes.label: docs/template/project-build/
nodes.summary: Project build template bundle — 9-step greenfield project setup flow with Spec/Plan/Impl/Test cycle.
nodes.name: docs/template/project-build

nodes.label: docs/template/project-build/flow.md
nodes.summary: Project build flow template (Template ID: project-build, Command: /pm-project-build, Category: development). 9-step FSM: S1 Understand Requirements → S2 Design Spec → S3 [HiL] Confirm Spec → S4 Design Plan → S5 [HiL] Review Plan → S6 Scaffold Project → S7 Core Implementation → S8 Write Tests → S9 Acceptance & Delivery. Includes package.json/tsconfig.json init, directory structure creation, build/test tooling setup. 2 HiL gates.
nodes.name: docs/template/project-build/flow.md

nodes.label: docs/template/research/
nodes.summary: Research template bundle — 7-step investigative flow for requirements gathering, exploration, and specification writing.
nodes.name: docs/template/research

nodes.label: docs/template/research/flow.md
nodes.summary: Research flow template (Template ID: research, Command: /pm-research, Category: research). 7-step FSM: S1 Understand Intent → S2 Explore Known Facts → S3 Mark Info Gaps → S4 [HiL] Progressive Interview (1 question at a time) → S5 Write Output (Spec/Tech Doc/Report) → S6 Review Output → S7 Revise & Finalize. Uses explore/librarian agents for discovery. Simple FSM with single HiL gate and back-edge from S6→S7 for revisions.
nodes.name: docs/template/research/flow.md

nodes.label: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for vibe-pm v0.1.0. Type: module (ESM), main: dist/index.js. Dependencies: axiodb ^9.6.6 (embedded structured memory), tiktoken ^1.0.22 (token counting), zod ^3.23.0 (runtime validation). DevDependencies: @opencode-ai/plugin ^1.17.4 (SDK), @types/node ^25.9.3, typescript ^5.5.0, vitest ^1.6.0. Scripts: build (tsc), test (vitest run), test:watch (vitest), typecheck (tsc --noEmit).
nodes.name: package.json

nodes.label: pnpm-lock.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm lockfile v9.0 with resolved dependency tree. Locks axiodb 9.6.6, tiktoken 1.0.22, zod 3.25.76, @opencode-ai/plugin 1.17.4, typescript 5.9.3, vitest 1.6.1 (with @types/node@25.9.3). Includes esbuild platform binaries, @rollup/rollup-*, @jridgewell/*, and other transitive dependencies.
nodes.name: pnpm-lock.yaml

nodes.label: rules/[rules]research.md
nodes.summary: Research task rules (v1.0.0) — a standalone flow definition in the rules/ directory serving as both a session rule and the original research flow template. 7-step FSM: S1 Understand Intent → S2 Explore Facts → S3 Mark Gaps → S4 [HiL] Progressive Interview (1 question at a time, with interview topic reference table covering goals/boundaries, scenarios/users, tech approach, priorities, risks) → S5 Design Plan (output to /docs/plan/) → S6 [HiL] Review Plan → S7 Execute Plan. More detailed than the research template with plan document structure specification, parallel task marking [P], and broader back-edge paths (S6→S5, S6→S4).
nodes.name: rules/[rules]research.md

nodes.label: FlowEngine
nodes.summary: Core business class implementing FSM step flow, three-layer sandwich context injection, message pruning pipeline, and LLM-driven step transitions. Located at src/engine/flow-engine.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements IFlowEngine interface: onMessage, injectContext, transformMessages, onSessionIdle, parseFlow, readFlowContent, listFlows, startTask, setStep.
nodes.name: FlowEngine

nodes.label: Three-Layer Sandwich Injection
nodes.summary: Context injection strategy splitting injected content into three layers: Layer 1 (global overview ~900T, cache-stable) with Constitution, FSM diagram, step summaries; Layer 2 (current step detail ~900T) with instructions, regulations, task state; Layer 3 (lookahead window 0-600T) with next 1-2 consecutive non-HiL steps. Saves 40-60% tokens vs full flow injection. Uses MD5 fingerprint deduplication to skip re-injection within same step. Designed to preserve LLM prompt cache stability.
nodes.name: SandwichInjection

nodes.label: Message Pruning Pipeline
nodes.summary: Three-step message pruning algorithm: (1) tagMessagesByStep — assigns step IDs to messages based on step transition timeline, (2) assignDepthLevel — allocates depth 0 (current, preserved), depth 1 (previous, key decisions), depth 2 (two steps back, user content only), depth 3 (older, placeholder), with HiL steps adding +1 depth to prior messages, (3) pruneByDepth — executes top-down until token budget met. Lazy: only triggers when tokens > 80% of maxStepTokens. Protection: user messages max depth 2, current step tool results never pruned, injected context never pruned, minimum 3 recent messages always kept.
nodes.name: MessagePruning

nodes.label: MemorySystem
nodes.summary: Data persistence class wrapping AxioDB embedded database (JSON file storage). Manages Task, Discussion, FlowMetrics CRUD operations. Located at src/memory/memory-system.ts.
nodes.name: MemorySystem

nodes.label: Task
nodes.summary: Core data model representing an active task: sessionId (PK), flow name, currentStep (S1-Sn), currentStepName, startAt (ISO 8601), closed boolean, summary, optional specRef and planRef. Managed by Memory System. Lifecycle: Created → Running → Completed.
nodes.name: Task

nodes.label: Discussion
nodes.summary: Auto-generated improvement suggestion from post-task analysis: UUID id, fromSessionId (FK→Task), priority (high/medium/low), importance and severity (1-5), issue/reason/solution descriptions, optional decision field (user-filled on async review), taskSummary (copied from Task), createdAt/resolvedAt timestamps.
nodes.name: Discussion

nodes.label: FlowMetrics
nodes.summary: Per-step runtime metrics: sessionId (FK), flow, step, stepName, stepInCount (entries), tokensConsumed, dwellTime (ms), humanInterventionTime (ms), userInputTokens, taskSummary. Collected on step enter/exit by Metrics & Analysis module. Aggregated per flow for trend analysis.
nodes.name: FlowMetrics

nodes.label: PluginCore
nodes.summary: Plugin entry point (VibePMPlugin function in src/core/plugin.ts). Orchestrates all modules: loads config, initializes Memory System and Flow Engine, registers 8 /pm-* commands via dual config+tool hooks, binds lifecycle hooks (chat.message, system.transform, messages.transform, session.idle). Module integration via direct imports — no DI container.
nodes.name: PluginCore

nodes.label: TemplateManager
nodes.summary: Template management functions (scanTemplates, installTemplate, uninstallFlow in src/template/template-manager.ts). Pure file operations by path convention — no abstraction layer. Scans /docs/template/, copies to /docs/flow/, auto-generates .opencode/commands/{command}.md prompt files from Command metadata field.
nodes.name: TemplateManager

nodes.label: FSM (Finite State Machine)
nodes.summary: Step transition model defined via Mermaid stateDiagram in flow documents. Transitions are NOT hardcoded — the Flow Engine injects the FSM diagram and instructions into the system prompt and lets the LLM autonomously decide when to advance steps. HiL (human-in-loop) steps require user confirmation before advancing; non-HiL steps auto-advance on completion. Back-edges enable iterative refinement.
nodes.name: FSM

nodes.label: Human-in-loop (HiL)
nodes.summary: Design pattern for steps requiring user intervention. Marked by ⚠️ emoji in step title + blockquote warning in flow docs. At these steps, the LLM must use blocking question/confirm tools, asking only 1 question at a time, and wait for user response before continuing. HiL steps serve as natural breakpoints for message pruning (prior steps get +1 depth level) and cache optimization (injection skipped during user interaction). Common HiL steps: plan review, spec confirmation, user acceptance.
nodes.name: HumanInLoop

nodes.label: AxioDB
nodes.summary: Embedded database v9.6.6 used for structured memory (JSON file storage at .vibe-pm/data.json). Key constraints: no $set operator (UpdateOne accepts plain objects), queries return { statusCode, data: { documents } } wrapper, single process instance limit. Stores three collections: tasks, discussions, flowMetrics.
nodes.name: AxioDB

nodes.label: TUI Display
nodes.summary: Terminal status display showing active tasks with flow name, current step, elapsed time, per-step dwell-time bar chart (with HiL markers and current-step highlight), and total token consumption. Read-only consumer of Memory System. Currently Draft — rendering approach TBD (Option A: OpenCode sidebar injection; Option B: independent ANSI process).
nodes.name: TUIDisplay

nodes.label: Metrics & Analysis
nodes.summary: Per-step metrics collection and post-task analysis module. Collects StepTokenBreakdown across 7 buckets, uses StickyTokenCache (5-min TTL) for debouncing, identifies bottlenecks via dwellTime>30%, stepInCount>3, or humanIntervention>50% thresholds, and auto-generates prioritized Discussion items. Deterministic rule-based analysis (non-LLM). Currently Draft.
nodes.name: MetricsAnalysis

nodes.label: Constitution
nodes.summary: Top-level governance with 8 non-negotiable principles: Flow+Spec+Test-Driven, Type Safety Supreme, Verification Mandatory, Mermaid Diagrams Only, Module Independence, Minimal Change, SDK-First Development, Chinese-First Communication. Always injected into system prompt (Layer 1). Zero-tolerance table defines enforcement for violations (type escape, skipping S4, deleting failing tests, mixing refactor into bugfix). Takes precedence over all other regulations.
nodes.name: Constitution

nodes.label: Coding Style
nodes.summary: TypeScript coding standard: UTF-8/LF, 2-space indent, Prettier formatting, kebab-case files, camelCase variables, PascalCase types/classes, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants. Strict type safety rules (no any/@ts-ignore/empty catch), function design guidelines (object params over 3 args, Result<T,E> pattern), async/await convention, structured logging, and TODO/FIXME/HACK placeholder format.
nodes.name: CodingStyle
Feature Spec Template
nodes.summary: Feature Spec template document defining the standard structure for feature specification documents, including sections for requirements, user stories, design points, boundaries, test cases, constraints, and development progress.

nodes.name: Complex Task Spec-Driven Development Flow
nodes.summary: A 12-step FSM-driven development process triggered when spec documents exist and requirements need adjustment. Covers understanding requirements, gap marking, progressive interviews, spec change design, confirmation, plan design, review, coding, testing, self-check, review confirmation, and delivery. Includes 4 human-in-loop checkpoints.

nodes.name: commands.ts
nodes.summary: Command registration module that declares all 8 /pm-* commands via config hook and registers executable tool implementations via tool hook using the @opencode-ai/plugin SDK.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Configuration management module that loads .vibe-pm.json from project directory, supports defaults, deep merging (especially for contextInjection), and auto-creates default config on startup.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export module for the plugin core layer, exposing VibePMPlugin, config functions, command registration, logger, and all core types.

nodes.name: logger.ts
nodes.summary: Console logger wrapper with unified [vibe-pm] prefix, implementing the ILogger interface. Zero external dependencies.

nodes.name: plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Main plugin entry function (VibePMPlugin). Orchestrates configuration loading, MemorySystem and FlowEngine initialization, and registration of all hooks: config commands, executable tools, chat.message, system.transform, messages.transform, and lifecycle events.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Core type definitions including PluginConfig (language, dataDir, contextInjection), IPluginContext, ILogger, ModuleHooks, and ModuleInit. Re-exports Plugin, PluginInput, Hooks, Config, ToolContext, ToolDefinition, and tool from the @opencode-ai/plugin SDK.

nodes.name: errors.ts
nodes.summary: Engine-specific error classes: DuplicateActiveTaskError (when starting task while one is active), FlowNotFoundError (missing flow document), FlowParseError (malformed flow document).

nodes.name: flow-engine.ts
nodes.summary: Core business logic layer: FlowEngine class. Parses Flow Markdown documents (extracting steps, FSM diagrams), builds sandwich-style context injection (3 layers: global overview, current step detail, lookahead window), prunes messages by token budget, coordinates step transitions, and manages task lifecycle.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export module for the engine layer, exposing FlowEngine, error classes (DuplicateActiveTaskError, FlowNotFoundError, FlowParseError), and flow-related types (FlowDefinition, StepDefinition, InjectionPlan, StartTaskParams).

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Engine type definitions: FlowDefinition (flow doc entity), StepDefinition (step with goal/agent/regulations/instructions), InjectionPlan (3-layer context), message pruning types (StepTaggedMessage, DepthAssignedMessage, StepTransition), and StartTaskParams.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Main package entry point that re-exports VibePMPlugin from the core barrel for OpenCode plugin loading.

nodes.name: errors.ts
nodes.summary: Memory system error classes: DuplicateTaskError (session already has active task) and MemorySystemError (generic data layer error).

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export module for the memory layer, exposing MemorySystem, error classes, and all memory-related types (Task, CreateTaskInput, Discussion, CreateDiscussionInput, FlowMetrics, IMemorySystem).

nodes.name: memory-system.ts
nodes.summary: Data persistence layer using AxioDB embedded JSON database. MemorySystem class manages three collections (tasks, discussions, flowMetrics) with full CRUD. Supports duplicate task detection, step tracking, discussion resolution, and flow metrics accumulation (step entries/exits with token and dwell-time tracking).

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Memory entity type definitions: Task (session task state), Discussion (improvement suggestion with priority/importance/severity), FlowMetrics (per-step metrics with entry count, tokens, dwell time), and the IMemorySystem interface contract.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export for the template manager, exposing scanTemplates, installTemplate, uninstallFlow, listInstalledFlows, TemplateConflictError, and TemplateMeta type.

nodes.name: template-manager.ts
nodes.summary: Pure file-operation module for template management. Scans /docs/template/ for flow templates, installs templates by copying flow.md + regulations to /docs/flow/ and /docs/regulation/, auto-generates .opencode/commands/ command files, and supports uninstallation with cleanup.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Template metadata type definition: TemplateMeta interface with id, name, category, description, version, command, flowPath, and bundleDir fields.

nodes.name: registerCommands
nodes.summary: Registers all 8 /pm-* slash commands as config hook declarations (non-executable, handled by plugin core). Mutates the opencodeConfig.commands record directly.

nodes.name: registerTools
nodes.summary: Creates executable tool registry from COMMANDS list using SDK tool() factory. Filters to executable commands only and creates stub implementations.

nodes.name: createStubTool
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates a stub ToolDefinition for an executable command, returning a placeholder message indicating the command is not yet implemented.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.summary: Loads and merges .vibe-pm.json configuration from the project directory with DEFAULT_CONFIG defaults. Handles deep merge for contextInjection and graceful fallback on parse errors.

nodes.name: writeConfig
nodes.summary: Writes PluginConfig to .vibe-pm.json with pretty-print formatting.

nodes.name: ensureDefaultConfig
nodes.summary: Ensures .vibe-pm.json exists; creates it with DEFAULT_CONFIG values if missing. Returns true if file was created.

nodes.name: VibePMPlugin
nodes.summary: Main async plugin entry function. Bootstraps configuration, initializes MemorySystem (AxioDB) and FlowEngine, then returns a Hooks object registering config commands, executable tools, chat message hooks, system transform for context injection, message transform for pruning, and session lifecycle events.

nodes.name: estimateTokens
nodes.summary: Estimates token count of a text string using tiktoken's cl100k_base encoding.

nodes.name: computeFingerprint
nodes.summary: Computes an MD5 fingerprint for a given flow-step-regulations combination used for injection deduplication.

nodes.name: shouldInject
nodes.summary: Checks whether context should be injected for a session by comparing the current fingerprint against the last injected fingerprint. Prevents redundant re-injection.

nodes.name: unwrapArray
nodes.summary: Unwraps an AxioDB result into an array of documents. Returns empty array on non-200 status or missing documents.

nodes.name: unwrapSingle
nodes.summary: Unwraps an AxioDB result into a single document. Returns null on non-200 status or missing documents.

nodes.name: generateId
nodes.summary: Generates a random UUID v4 using crypto.randomUUID().

nodes.name: getDocsDir
nodes.summary: Returns the docs/ directory path within the project directory.

nodes.name: getCommandsDir
nodes.summary: Returns the .opencode/commands/ directory path within the project directory.

nodes.name: stripLeadingSlash
nodes.summary: Strips a leading slash from a command name string.

nodes.name: parseTemplateMeta
nodes.summary: Parses template metadata from a flow.md file's frontmatter-style fields: Template ID, name, Category, Description, Version, and Command. Returns a TemplateMeta object or null if ID/name are missing.

nodes.name: parseFlowScenario
nodes.summary: Extracts the Scenario section content from a flow.md document.

nodes.name: parseFlowInputReqs
nodes.summary: Parses the input requirements table from a flow.md document into structured {name, required, description} entries.

nodes.name: generateCommandFile
nodes.summary: Generates the Markdown content for a .opencode/commands/ command file from template metadata and flow content, including scenario, input requirements table, and execution steps.

nodes.name: scanTemplates
nodes.summary: Scans the /docs/template/ directory for flow template bundles (subdirectories containing flow.md), parses their metadata, and returns a list of available templates.

nodes.name: installTemplate
nodes.summary: Installs a flow template by copying flow.md to /docs/flow/, copying regulation files to /docs/regulation/, and auto-generating a command file in .opencode/commands/. Throws TemplateConflictError if the flow already exists.

nodes.name: uninstallFlow
nodes.summary: Removes an installed flow document from /docs/flow/ and cleans up the corresponding command file in .opencode/commands/. Tries both [flow]name.md and name.md patterns.

nodes.name: listInstalledFlows
nodes.summary: Lists all installed flows by reading the /docs/flow/ directory and stripping the [flow] prefix and .md extension from filenames.

nodes.name: DuplicateActiveTaskError
nodes.summary: Thrown when attempting to start a new task while an active task already exists in the current session. Provides details about the existing task.

nodes.name: FlowNotFoundError
nodes.summary: Thrown when a requested Flow document is not found in /docs/flow/.

nodes.name: FlowParseError
nodes.summary: Thrown when a Flow document fails to parse correctly.

nodes.name: FlowEngine
nodes.summary: Central business logic class. Provides hook callbacks (onMessage, injectContext, transformMessages, onSessionIdle), flow management (parseFlow, readFlowContent, listFlows), task operations (startTask, setStep, getCurrentStep), an embedded Markdown parser for flow documents, 3-layer context injection (global overview + current step + lookahead), and message pruning by token budget.

nodes.name: DuplicateTaskError
nodes.summary: Thrown when attempting to create a second Task for a session that already has an active task. Data-layer level enforcement of one-active-task-per-session constraint.

nodes.name: MemorySystemError
nodes.summary: Generic data-layer error for the MemorySystem.

nodes.name: MemorySystem
nodes.summary: Primary data persistence class implementing IMemorySystem. Manages three AxioDB collections: tasks (FSM task state), discussions (improvement items), flowMetrics (per-step analytics). Provides full CRUD with duplicate task detection, discussion resolution tracking, and accumulative flow metrics recording.

nodes.name: TemplateConflictError
nodes.summary: Thrown when attempting to install a flow template that already exists in /docs/flow/. Suggests using --force to overwrite.

nodes.title: Commands Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for command registration and tool registration — verifies 8 declarative /pm-* commands and 4 executable runtime tools are registered correctly, tests duplicate-key overwrite semantics, and validates stub tool placeholder responses.
nodes.content: Two describe blocks: `registerCommands` (tests all 8 /pm-* commands, duplicate overwrite) and `registerTools` (tests 4 executable tools: pm_task_start/set_step/refresh/close, verifies stubs return [vibe-pm] placeholder messages). Asserts command metadata includes template, description, and agent fields.
nodes.name: Commands Test

nodes.title: Config Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for configuration management — validates loadConfig default fallback, partial overrides with deep merge of contextInjection, invalid JSON graceful degradation, ensureDefaultConfig creation/skip semantics, and writeConfig persistence.
nodes.content: Uses real filesystem ops (mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, rmSync) to test config lifecycle. Two describe blocks: `loadConfig` (4 cases: defaults, partial override, invalid JSON → fallback, deep merge of contextInjection) and `ensureDefaultConfig` (2 cases: create when missing, skip when exists). Mocks logger module via vi.mock.

dependencies.detail: Mocks logger calls; verifies warn/info side-effects

nodes.name: Config Test

nodes.title: Plugin Test
nodes.summary: Vitest integration test for the VibePMPlugin bootstrap — validates all 6 hook registrations (config, tool, chat.message, chat.system.transform, chat.messages.transform, event), passthrough behavior without active tasks, and session lifecycle event handling.
nodes.content: Creates a single shared Plugin instance via beforeAll for the whole file. Asserts 6 hooks exist as functions/objects. Verifies chat.message passthrough when no active task, session.created and session.idle events resolve without throwing. Uses a minimal PluginInput mock.

dependencies.detail: Integration-tests VibePMPlugin bootstrap and hook registration

nodes.name: Plugin Test

nodes.title: Context Injection Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for FlowEngine.context injection — validates triple-layer output (constitution + FSM diagram + step overview + current step + task state + FSM instructions), Layer 3 lookahead-window for non-HiL steps, and no-op injection when no active task or no sessionID.
nodes.content: Creates a test project with docs/flow/test-flow.md and regulation files (constitution.md, coding_style.md) copied from the real project. Initializes MemorySystem + FlowEngine. Tests injectContext output contains expected XML-like markers (<constitution>, <fsm-diagram>, <step-overview>, <current-step>, <task-state>, <lookahead-window>). Verifies passthrough when sessionID is missing.
nodes.name: Context Injection Test

nodes.title: Flow Parser Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for FlowEngine flow document parsing — validates complete flow parsing (name, command, steps count, FSM diagram), step field correctness (id, name, goal, agent, instructions, humanInLoop, onComplete), HiL step detection, FSM diagram extraction, missing-flow error, flow listing, and raw content reading.
nodes.content: Uses test-flow.md (4-step FSM: S1理解需求 → S2标记缺口 → S3审查计划(HiL) → S4执行实施). Tests parseFlow for step completeness, FlowNotFoundError for nonexistent flows, listFlows and readFlowContent. Validates step S3 has humanInLoop=true.
nodes.name: Flow Parser Test

nodes.title: Message Pruner Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for FlowEngine message pruning — validates that pruning is skipped when disabled (pruneIrrelevant=false), user messages are retained during pruning, old assistant messages are replaced with placeholder text, and no pruning occurs when no active task exists.
nodes.content: Configures PRUNE_CONFIG with maxStepTokens=1000 and pruneIrrelevant=true. Tests transformMessages with artificial message arrays (10–20 messages) to verify trimming behavior. Validates the `[前置步骤消息已裁剪]` placeholder is injected for pruned messages, user messages survive pruning, and output.message count is >0 even after aggressive trimming.
nodes.name: Message Pruner Test

nodes.title: Task Start Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for FlowEngine task lifecycle — validates task creation (sessionId binding, automatic S1 assignment, closed=false), duplicate active task rejection (DuplicateActiveTaskError), missing-flow error (FlowNotFoundError), and manual step jumping via setStep.
nodes.content: Tests startTask returning the full task object with correct flow, currentStep=S1, and summary fields. Validates DuplicateActiveTaskError when a session tries to start a second task, FlowNotFoundError for nonexistent flows, and setStep successfully jumping to S3 and updating currentStep and currentStepName.
nodes.name: Task Start Test

nodes.title: Data File Test
nodes.summary: Smoke test for MemorySystem initialization — verifies AxioDB creates its data directory on first run (vibe-pm/vibe-pm/ subdirectory) and that CRUD operations (createTask, getTask) function correctly immediately after init.
nodes.content: Minimal test — init creates vibe-pm/ directory with nested vibe-pm/ AxioDB app dir, then validates that createTask + getTask round-trip works on the freshly initialized database. Uses temp directories with auto-cleanup via afterAll rmSync.
nodes.name: Data File Test

nodes.title: Discussion CRUD Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for Discussion CRUD operations on MemorySystem — verifies auto-fill of taskSummary from associated task, retrieval of unresolved-only discussions, resolve operation setting decision+resolvedAt, and listDiscussions filtering by priority and unresolved status.
nodes.content: Tests createDiscussion (priority high/medium, importance, severity, issue/reason/solution fields), getUnresolvedDiscussions, resolveDiscussion (partial/accept decisions), getDiscussions by session, and listDiscussions with filters. Validates that creating a Discussion against an existing task auto-fills taskSummary from that task.

dependencies.detail: Unit-tests createDiscussion, getUnresolvedDiscussions, resolveDiscussion, getDiscussions, listDiscussions

nodes.name: Discussion CRUD Test

nodes.title: FlowMetrics CRUD Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for FlowMetrics per-step telemetry — validates recordStepEntry/recordStepExit capture tokensConsumed, dwellTime, humanInterventionTime, stepInCount accumulation on re-entry, aggregation by flow name via getFlowMetricsByFlow, and cumulative exit metrics across multiple exits.
nodes.content: Tests recordStepEntry with sessionId, flow, step, stepName, tokensConsumed, userInputTokens. Tests recordStepExit with dwellTime, humanInterventionTime. Validates stepInCount increments on re-entry (1→2), token sums accumulate (1000+2000=3000), dwellTime accumulates across multiple exits (10000+20000=30000). Tests getFlowMetrics aggregates by session, getFlowMetricsByFlow aggregates by flow name.
nodes.name: FlowMetrics CRUD Test

nodes.title: Task CRUD Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for Task CRUD operations on MemorySystem — validates task creation with all fields, getActiveTask filtering (open vs closed), updateStep syncs currentStep+currentStepName, duplicate session rejection via DuplicateTaskError, and listActiveTasks aggregation.
nodes.content: Tests createTask (sessionId, flow, currentStep, currentStepName, startAt, summary, closed), getTask fetching, getActiveTask returning null for closed tasks, updateStep atomic step name+id update, duplicate task prevention, and listActiveTasks showing multiple concurrent sessions. Uses a baseTask factory for concise test setup.

dependencies.detail: Unit-tests createTask, getTask, getActiveTask, updateStep, closeTask, listActiveTasks
Imports DuplicateTaskError for duplicate rejection assertion

nodes.name: Task CRUD Test

nodes.title: Command File Generation Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for automatic OpenCode command file generation during template install — validates TemplateMeta.command field parsing, installTemplate creating .opencode/commands/<command>.md with proper structure, uninstallFlow cleaning up command files, and generated content including title, scenario, input requirements table, and execution steps.
nodes.content: Comprehensive test of command file lifecycle. Helper functions writeTemplateWithCommand (creates templates with **Command** metadata and optional input requirements table) and writeTemplateWithoutCommand (no command field). Tests: meta parsing includes command, null command for templates without it, install creates .opencode/commands/pm-*.md with full structure (title, scenario, task-start section pointing to /pm-task-start, input requirements table, execution steps), skip generation when no command, uninstall removes both flow file and command file.

dependencies.detail: Unit-tests scanTemplates, installTemplate, uninstallFlow with command generation focus
TemplateMeta type validated via scanTemplates return value

nodes.name: Command File Generation Test

nodes.title: Template Scan Test
nodes.summary: Vitest test for template management operations — validates scanTemplates discovers all template bundles, parses metadata (id, name, category, version), skips directories without flow.md, installTemplate copies flow.md and companion regulation files to target dirs, uninstallFlow removes installed flows, and conflict detection on overwrite.
nodes.content: Tests the full template lifecycle: scan (finds all, parses meta, skips empty dirs), install (copies flow.md as [flow]<id>.md to docs/flow/, copies regulations/ to docs/regulation/ with overwrite protection for existing files), uninstall (removes target file, preserves other flows via listInstalledFlows), and TemplateConflictError on double-install. Helper functions create template bundle directories with flow.md and optional regulations/ subdirectory.

dependencies.detail: Unit-tests scanTemplates, installTemplate, uninstallFlow, listInstalledFlows, TemplateConflictError
TemplateMeta type validated through scanTemplates returns

nodes.name: Template Scan Test

nodes.title: TypeScript Configuration
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration targeting ES2022 with NodeNext module resolution, strict mode enabled, outputs to dist/ with declarations and source maps, sources from src/.
nodes.content: compilerOptions: target=ES2022, module=NodeNext, moduleResolution=NodeNext, strict=true, esModuleInterop=true, skipLibCheck=true, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames=true, outDir=./dist, rootDir=./src, declaration=true, sourceMap=true. Includes src/**/*.ts, excludes node_modules and dist.
nodes.name: TypeScript Configuration

nodes.title: Vitest Configuration
nodes.summary: Vitest test runner configuration with a single test include pattern matching tests/**/*.test.ts, using the default Vitest config export pattern.
nodes.content: Exports a default defineConfig with test.include set to ["tests/**/*.test.ts"]. Simple configuration — all test files live under tests/ and follow the *.test.ts naming convention.
nodes.name: Vitest Configuration

layers.name: Plugin Core
layers.description: Plugin entry point, lifecycle orchestration, slash-command registration, configuration loading/merging, logging, and core type definitions. Integrates all other modules via direct TypeScript imports (no DI container).

layers.name: Flow Engine
layers.description: Core business logic: LLM-driven FSM state machine for step transitions, three-layer sandwich context injection (global overview + current step + lookahead window), three-pass message pruning pipeline (step-tagging → depth-level assignment → token-constrained execution), flow document parsing, and task lifecycle management.

layers.name: Memory System
layers.description: Data persistence layer wrapping AxioDB embedded JSON database. Manages three collections (tasks, discussions, flowMetrics) with full CRUD operations, duplicate task detection, discussion resolution tracking, and accumulative per-step metrics recording.

layers.name: Template Manager
layers.description: Pure file-operation module for flow template lifecycle: scanning /docs/template/ for template bundles, installing templates (copying flow.md to /docs/flow/, regulations to /docs/regulation/), auto-generating .opencode/commands/ command files from template metadata, and uninstalling with cleanup. No abstraction layer — path conventions only.

layers.name: Specifications & Design Docs
layers.description: Authoritative design documents — the source of truth before code changes. Covers overall system architecture, per-module specs (Plugin Core, Flow Engine, Memory System, Template Manager, Metrics & Analysis, TUI Display), project roadmap, and top-level AGENTS.md serving as onboarding and code-map reference. Highest documentation tier in the regulation hierarchy.

layers.name: Governance & Session Rules
layers.description: Project governance and behavioral rules loaded per OpenCode session. Constitution (8 non-negotiable principles), coding style regulation (TypeScript conventions), terminology dictionary (Chinese-English mappings), and three session rules defining research, spec-driven development, and spec-template behaviors. Constitution takes supremacy in regulation hierarchy.

layers.name: Flow Templates
layers.description: Built-in and installed flow definitions consumed by the Flow Engine. 5 template bundles (research 7-step, project-build 9-step, new-feature 13-step, bug-fix 10-step, large-refactor 14-step) each containing a flow.md with Mermaid stateDiagram FSM, plus a flow-template.md reference document and installed flows under docs/flow/. Templates include bundled regulations and Command metadata for auto-generated slash commands.

layers.name: Build & Configuration
layers.description: Project-level build, packaging, and tooling configuration. Package manifest with dependencies and scripts, TypeScript compiler config (ES2022 target, NodeNext module, strict mode), Vitest test runner config, pnpm lockfile pinning exact dependency versions, and gitignore excluding runtime data and build artifacts.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Vitest test suites mirroring the src/ directory structure: core tests (plugin bootstrap, command registration, config loading), engine tests (context injection layers, flow parsing, message pruning, task lifecycle), memory tests (task CRUD, discussion CRUD, flow metrics CRUD, data file initialization), and template tests (template scanning, installation, uninstallation, command file generation). Uses real AxioDB instances and filesystem operations — no mocking per project conventions.

tour.title: Project Overview: What is vibe-pm?
tour.description: Start with the top-level AGENTS.md to understand vibe-pm's purpose, core concepts, tech stack, and design philosophy. This document is the onboarding entry point — it explains the dual-layer memory system (MD docs + AxioDB structured memory), the FSM-driven task model with LLM-judged state transitions, step-aware context injection with message pruning, per-step metrics collection, and the anti-patterns to avoid.
tour.languageLesson: Understanding how a plugin orchestrates its modules through direct TypeScript imports (no DI container) — a pattern common in small-to-medium TypeScript projects where dependency graphs are shallow and explicit.

tour.title: System Architecture: The 6-Layer Design
tour.description: Dive into the overall system design spec to understand the 6-layer architecture: Plugin Core (entry/lifecycle), Flow Engine (FSM/context), Memory System (AxioDB JSON), Metrics & Analysis, TUI Display, and Template Manager. See how modules compose through direct imports, how data flows between layers (write vs read paths), and the security policy (read-only file ops, no deletes, no network). The roadmap shows a 4-phase delivery plan from MVP core to v1.0 release.
tour.languageLesson: Recognizing how a plugin architecture maps to a layered design: entry point → business logic → persistence, with orthogonal concerns (templates, metrics, TUI) fanning out from the core layers.

tour.title: Governance & Constitution: The Rules That Shape the Code
tour.description: Explore the project's governance layer — three regulation documents that are always injected into the system prompt (Layer 1 of context injection). The Constitution defines 8 non-negotiable principles (Flow+Spec+Test-Driven, Type Safety Supreme, Verification Mandatory, Mermaid Diagrams Only, Module Independence, Minimal Change, SDK-First, Chinese-First Communication). The Coding Style regulation enforces strict TypeScript conventions (no any, no @ts-ignore, no empty catch). The Dictionary provides Chinese-English terminology mappings for all core concepts.
tour.languageLesson: How a project enforces coding standards through a constitution-style document rather than just linter configs — this is especially effective for AI-assisted development where principles need to be explicit in system prompts.

tour.title: Plugin Core: Entry Point & Command Registration
tour.description: Walk through the Plugin Core layer — the entry point that bootstraps everything. VibePMPlugin (src/core/plugin.ts) orchestrates configuration loading, MemorySystem and FlowEngine initialization, and hooks registration. The commands module (src/core/commands.ts) registers all 8 /pm-* slash commands via dual config+tool hooks using the @opencode-ai/plugin SDK. Config management (src/core/config.ts) handles .vibe-pm.json with deep merging, defaults, and graceful degradation on corruption.
tour.languageLesson: The @opencode-ai/plugin SDK pattern: a plugin exports a single async function that receives PluginInput and returns a Hooks object. Hook keys map to OpenCode lifecycle events — config for command declarations, tool for executable implementations, chat.* for message interception.

tour.title: Memory System: AxioDB Persistence Layer
tour.description: Explore the data persistence layer built on AxioDB v9.6.6 — an embedded JSON-file database. The MemorySystem class manages three collections: tasks (FSM task state with lifecycle: Created→Running→Completed), discussions (auto-generated improvement suggestions with priority 1-5 and resolution tracking), and flowMetrics (per-step telemetry: entry count, tokens consumed, dwell time, human intervention time). Key AxioDB constraints: UpdateOne uses plain objects (no $set operator), queries return a { statusCode, data: { documents } } wrapper, and only one process can access the database at a time.
tour.languageLesson: Working with embedded databases in TypeScript: understanding the response envelope pattern (statusCode + data.documents), the plain-object update convention (no MongoDB-style operators), and single-process limitation — all constraints discovered through hands-on testing rather than documentation.

tour.title: Flow Engine: FSM, Context Injection & Message Pruning
tour.description: The heart of the plugin — the FlowEngine class (668 lines) implements all core business logic. It parses Flow Markdown documents (extracting steps, FSM diagrams, instructions), builds the three-layer sandwich context injection (Layer 1: global overview with Constitution + FSM + step summaries, ~900 tokens; Layer 2: current step detail with instructions + regulations + task state, ~900 tokens; Layer 3: lookahead window for next 1-2 non-HiL steps, 0-600 tokens), and runs the three-pass message pruning pipeline (step-tagging → depth-level assignment → token-constrained execution with protection mechanisms). Injection uses MD5 fingerprint deduplication to skip re-injection within the same step. The FSM is LLM-driven — the engine injects the state diagram and lets the LLM autonomously decide step transitions, with HiL (human-in-loop) steps requiring user confirmation.
tour.languageLesson: Designing a token-aware context injection system: understanding prompt cache stability (Layer 1 changes rarely, so the LLM can cache it), fingerprint-based deduplication, lazy pruning thresholds, and protection mechanisms that prevent critical context from being pruned. The three-layer sandwich pattern is applicable to any system that needs to inject varying amounts of context into LLM prompts.

tour.title: Template Manager: Install, Uninstall & Command Generation
tour.description: Learn how flow templates are managed through pure file operations by path convention — no abstraction layer. The template-manager.ts module scans /docs/template/ for template bundles (subdirectories with flow.md), parses their metadata (Template ID, Category, Command, Version), installs them by copying flow.md to /docs/flow/ and regulations to /docs/regulation/, and auto-generates .opencode/commands/ command files from the **Command** metadata field. Uninstallation cleans up both the flow file and command file. The TemplateConflictError prevents accidental overwrites unless --force is used.
tour.languageLesson: File-based plugin extension patterns: how to auto-generate command prompt files from template metadata, parsing frontmatter-style fields from Markdown, and implementing clean uninstallation that removes everything installed (flow docs + command files) without leaving orphaned artifacts.

tour.title: Flow Templates: The 5 Built-in Workflows
tour.description: Explore the 5 built-in flow templates that ship with vibe-pm, each defining a complete FSM-driven workflow with Mermaid stateDiagram, numbered steps, and HiL gates. Research (7 steps): investigate → interview → write output. Project Build (9 steps): spec → scaffold → core impl → deliver. New Feature (13 steps, 4 HiL gates): the most comprehensive, mirroring XMind's heavy-task workflow with progressive clarification. Bug Fix (10 steps): root cause → minimal fix → regression → merge. Large Refactor (14 steps, 3 HiL gates): the largest template, with impact assessment, backward compatibility via Adapter/Facade, and a bundled migration-checklist regulation for S10 Compatibility Verification. The flow-template.md defines the canonical format all templates follow.
tour.languageLesson: Designing reusable workflow templates: understanding how FSM diagrams encode process logic, how HiL gates create natural breakpoints for human review, and how back-edges enable iterative refinement. The template format balances structure (fixed sections) with flexibility (variable step counts, optional bundled regulations).

tour.title: Tests: 13 Suites Across 4 Subsystems
tour.description: Walk through the test layer — 13 Vitest suites that mirror the src/ directory exactly. Core tests (3 suites): plugin bootstrap with hook verification, command registration completeness, and config loading with deep merge and corruption handling. Engine tests (4 suites): context injection layer validation, flow document parsing (steps, FSM diagram, HiL detection), message pruning with token budget enforcement, and task lifecycle (create, duplicate rejection, setStep). Memory tests (4 suites): task CRUD with duplicate detection, discussion CRUD with resolution tracking, flow metrics CRUD with accumulative telemetry, and data file initialization smoke test. Template tests (2 suites): template scanning and installation, plus dedicated command file generation lifecycle. All tests use real AxioDB instances and filesystem operations — no mocking per project conventions.
tour.languageLesson: Testing patterns for embedded databases and filesystem operations: using temp directories with auto-cleanup, testing AxioDB response envelope unwrapping, validating accumulative metrics (stepInCount increments, dwellTime sums), and enforcing anti-pattern rules (no mocking of AxioDB internals).

tour.title: Build & Configuration: Tooling & Dependencies
tour.description: Wrap up the tour with the project's build and configuration layer. package.json defines vibe-pm v0.1.0 as an ESM module (type: module) with three runtime dependencies: axiodb for embedded structured memory, tiktoken for cl100k_base token counting, and zod for runtime validation. Dev dependencies include the @opencode-ai/plugin SDK and vitest for testing. TypeScript targets ES2022 with NodeNext module resolution and strict mode enabled. Vitest is configured with a simple include pattern matching tests/**/*.test.ts. The .gitignore excludes node_modules, dist, .vibe-pm runtime data, and generated docs/flow and docs/regulation directories.
tour.languageLesson: Configuring a modern TypeScript ESM project: understanding the NodeNext module resolution strategy, why type: module in package.json matters, and how vitest's simple include pattern keeps test configuration minimal when test files follow a consistent naming convention.
